# Test MCP tools integration

# Create test files
exec echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"init","id":1} # 1000.000' > test1.mcp
exec echo 'mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{}} # 1001.000' >> test1.mcp
exec echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","id":2} # 1002.000' >> test1.mcp
exec echo 'mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"message":"pong"}} # 1003.000' >> test1.mcp

# Test mcpcat
exec mcpcat test1.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv'
stdout 'mcp-send'

# Test mcp-sort (strip timestamps)
exec mcp-sort -strip test1.mcp
stdout '\[TIMESTAMP\]'

# Create a second file for diff
exec cp test1.mcp test2.mcp
exec echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"new","id":3} # 1004.000' >> test2.mcp

# Test mcpdiff
exec mcpdiff test1.mcp test2.mcp
stdout 'new'

# Test mcpspy with a simple echo command
exec echo 'test' | mcpspy -f spy.mcp echo
stdout 'test'
exists spy.mcp
